---
output:
  pdf_document:
    keep_tex: false
    extra_dependencies: ["float"]
params: 
  state: ""    
title: "`r params$state` Summary Sheet"
author: "Oregon Bee Atlas"


geometry: margin=2cm
header-includes:
- \usepackage{booktabs}
- \usepackage{longtable}
- \usepackage{array}
- \usepackage{multirow}
- \usepackage{wrapfig}
- \usepackage{float}
- \usepackage{colortbl}
- \usepackage{pdflscape}
- \usepackage{tabu}
- \usepackage{threeparttable}
- \usepackage{threeparttablex}
- \usepackage[normalem]{ulem}
- \usepackage{makecell}
- \usepackage{xcolor}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#OREGON BEE ATLAS STATE SUMMARY PDFs
#WRITTEN BY ROWAN RAMPTON 2025, adapted from Sam Robinson Project Summary script
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(sf)
library(ggspatial) #library(ggsn) replaced
library(shadowtext)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(iNEXT) 
library(cowplot)

#knitr options
opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, eval = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
opts_knit$set(eval.after = "fig.cap")
options(dplyr.summarise.inform=FALSE, warn=-1) #Suppress warnings
theme_set(theme_classic())
```

```{r flora list, include=FALSE}
# uses this list to check oregon species for exoticness https://oregonflora.org/pages/taxonomic-checklist.php

orFlora <- read_csv("data/OR_FloraList.csv", locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")) %>% 
  rename(plantGenSpp = Scientific_name) %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(plantGenSpp, names = c("gen", "spp"), delim = " ", too_many = "merge", cols_remove = FALSE) %>%
  separate_wider_delim(cols = Origin, names = c("orig1", "orig2"), delim = ", ", too_many = "merge", too_few = "align_start")

genusCertainty <- orFlora %>% filter(!is.na(orig1)) %>% group_by(gen) %>% 
  summarise(origins = paste0(unique(orig1), collapse = "___"), 
            n = n_distinct(orig1)) %>% 
  mutate(origins = case_when(
    n == 1 ~ origins, 
    n!= 1 ~ "Uncertain"
  ))

# specialist bee list

specBeesLookup <- read_csv("Robinson/Data/specialistBees.csv") %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(cols = `Genus (Subgenus) species`, delim = " ", names = c("genus", "subgenusSpp"), too_few = "align_start", too_many = "merge") %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(subgenusSpp, ") ", names = c("subgen", "spp"), too_few = "align_end") %>% 
  mutate(genSpp = str_c(genus, " ", spp))


# datFlora <- dat %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% left_join(orFlora %>% filter(Name_Status %in% "Accepted")) %>% 
#   left_join(genusCertainty %>% select(!n)) %>% 
#   mutate(orig1 = case_when(
#     !is.na(orig1) ~ orig1, 
#     is.na(orig1) ~ origins
#   ))

below15_proj <- dat %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(genSpp, "spp\\.|Bombus impatiens")) %>% 
  group_by(genSpp) %>% 
  count() %>% 
  filter(n<15) %>% ungroup()
```


```{r setup datSt, include=FALSE}
# fix ecoregion names in ecoregsimplified

ecoRegS_namesForState <- ecoRegSimplified %>% 
  mutate(name = str_replace_all(name, "&", "and"), 
         name = str_replace_all(name, "\\n", " "))

# filter to state

datSt <- dat %>% 
   st_join(ecoRegS_namesForState %>% select(STUSPS, geometry), st_within) %>% 
  filter(STUSPS%in% params$state) %>% # fixes the out of state points for now
select(!STUSPS) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(genSpp)) %>% # no missing bee IDs currently - this is built into downstream code already 
  left_join(orFlora %>% filter(Name_Status %in% "Accepted") %>% select(plantGenSpp, orig1, gen)) %>% 
  left_join(genusCertainty %>% select(!n)) %>%# add flora list - if species don't match, they get assigned a genus level
  mutate(orig1 = case_when( 
    !is.na(orig1) ~ orig1, 
    is.na(orig1) ~ origins # genus level status can be uncertain (both native and exotic in genus)
  )) %>% 
  mutate(orig1 = case_when(
    plantGenSpp %in% "Rubus bifrons" ~ "exotic",
    is.na(orig1) ~ "mixed" ,
    .default = orig1
  ))


```


```{r setup fxns, include=FALSE}
## Statewide Summary Setup ----

## Functions ---- 

# OR use params for state? Simplifies function, allows multiple plots
addChao2State <- function(df, state2do, varName = county){ # default is county, use "name" for ecoreg
  
  datMat <- df %>% 
    filter(state %in% state2do) %>% # only one state at a time
    st_drop_geometry() %>% 
    select({{varName}}, genSpp) %>% 
    filter(!is.na(genSpp)) %>% 
    group_by({{varName}}, genSpp) %>% 
    count() %>% 
    pivot_wider(values_from = n, names_from = {{varName}}, values_fill = 0) %>% 
    column_to_rownames(var = "genSpp") # make into matrix with each col a county, rows are spp
  
  if(as.character(ensym(varName)) == "county"){
  chaoDat <- as_tibble(iNEXT::ChaoRichness(datMat) %>% # turn output into workable format
                         rownames_to_column(var = "altName")) %>% # altname = county 
    rename_with(make.names, everything()) # fix names
  
  countyRichEst <- usCountiesW %>% filter(STUSPS %in% state2do) %>% 
    left_join(chaoDat, by = c("altName")) # Add to state boundaries
  return(countyRichEst)
  
  }else
  {
   chaoDat <- as_tibble(iNEXT::ChaoRichness(datMat) %>% # turn output into workable format
                         rownames_to_column(var = "name")) %>% # name = ecoreg 
    rename_with(make.names, everything()) 
   
   countyRichEst <- stateEcoReg %>% filter(STUSPS %in% state2do) %>% 
    left_join(chaoDat, by = c("name")) # Add to state boundaries
  return(countyRichEst)
  }
  
}

```


```{r setup map plot fxns, include=FALSE}
actualStateRich <- function(df){
  return(ggplot(df)+
    geom_sf(aes(fill = Observed))
    
    )
  #+)
  #geom_shadowtext() # add number actual 
}

estStateRich <- function(df){
  return(ggplot(df)+
           geom_sf(aes(fill = Estimator))
         
         )
  #+)
  #geom_shadowtext() # add number actual 
}

gapStateRich <- function(df){
  return(ggplot(df)+
           geom_sf(aes(fill = (Estimator-Observed)))+
    labs(fill = "Est. - Obs.")
  )
  #+)
  #geom_shadowtext() # add number actual 
}

gapStateRich_focus <- function(df){
  return(ggplot(df)+
           geom_sf(aes(fill = (Estimator-Observed)), alpha = 0)+
    labs(fill = "Est. - Obs.")
  ) 
}
```

```{r setup chao state, include=FALSE}
#debugonce(addChao2State)
stateChao <- addChao2State(datSt, state2do = params$state )
```


```{r setup - ecoreg, include=FALSE}
## ecoregion summary setup ---- 

datEcoR <- datSt %>% # match ecoreg to pnts
  st_join(ecoRegS_namesForState, st_within) %>% 
    mutate(name = str_replace_all(name, "\\n", " "), 
           name = str_replace_all(name, "&" , "and")) # fixing names that cause problems later  
#%>% filter(STUSPS%in% params$state) # fixes the out of state points for now

# get only ecoregions within state 
stateEcoReg <- ecoRegS_namesForState %>% 
  filter(STUSPS%in% params$state) %>% 
  group_by(L3_KEY) %>% 
  mutate(ecorArea = st_area(geometry))

# some are just outside (showing up in water probably, or minor CRS diffs) - for now just used st_nearest, might not be perfect
datEcoR_nearest <- datSt %>% semi_join(datEcoR %>% filter(is.na(L3_KEY)) %>% st_drop_geometry()) %>% 
  st_join(ecoRegS_namesForState, st_nearest_feature) # %>% filter(is.na(L3_KEY))

datEcoR <- datEcoR %>% # add ones back in that needed it
  anti_join(datEcoR_nearest %>% st_drop_geometry(), 
            join_by(collector, day, month, year, verbatimEventDate, country, state, county, locality, phylumPlant, orderPlant,
                    familyPlant, genusPlant, plantGenSpp, taxonRankPlant, familyVolDet, genusVolDet, specificEpithetVolDet, sexVolDet, casteVolDet, genus,
                    species, taxonomist, sex, caste, samplingProtocol, date, volunteerGenSpp, genSpp, family)) %>% 
  rbind(datEcoR_nearest)

datIded2taxa <- datSt %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(family) %>% 
  count()
```

```{r setup - percent identified figures, include=FALSE}
generaID <- dat %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(family, genus, species) %>% 
  count() %>% ungroup() %>%  
  mutate(sppLev = case_when(species %in% NA ~ "Not Identified", !is.na(species) ~ "Identified")) %>% 
  group_by(family, genus, sppLev) %>% 
  summarise(n = sum(n)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(family))

familyInt <- 
 dat %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(family, genus, species, year) %>% 
  count() %>% ungroup() %>%  
  mutate(sppLev = case_when(species %in% NA ~ "Not Identified", !is.na(species) ~ "Identified")) %>% 
  group_by(family, genus, sppLev, year) %>% 
  summarise(n = sum(n)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(family))

familyLevel <- familyInt %>% 
  group_by(family, sppLev)  %>% 
  summarise(n = sum(n)) %>% 
  mutate(famID = str_c(family, " - ", sppLev))

familyLevYr <- familyInt %>% 
  group_by(family, sppLev, year)  %>% 
  summarise(n = sum(n)) %>% 
  mutate(famID = str_c(family, " - ", sppLev))

famCols <- tibble(cols=c(brewer.pal(5,'Set1'), "black"), 
                  fams = c('Andrenidae','Apidae','Colletidae',
                           'Halictidae','Megachilidae', "Melittidae"))
idFamCols <- famCols %>% 
  mutate(sppLev = "Identified") %>%  
  rbind(famCols %>% mutate(sppLev = "Not Identified", cols = "darkgrey")) %>% 
  mutate(famID = str_c(fams, " - ", sppLev)) %>% select(!c(fams, sppLev))

famCols <- as.character(idFamCols$cols)
names(famCols) <- idFamCols$famID

genusIdedPlot <- generaID %>% 
  mutate(famID = str_c(family, " - ", sppLev)) %>% 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = genus, y = n, fill = famID)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = T))+
  facet_wrap(~family, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90 , vjust = 0.5, hjust=1), legend.position = "none")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = famCols)+
  labs(x = "Genus", y = "Percentage of Genus Identified to Species")
  

familyIdedPlot <- familyLevel %>% 
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = family, y = n, fill = famID)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = T))+
  #facet_wrap(~family, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2)+
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90 , vjust = 0.5, hjust=1))+
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = famCols)+
  labs(y = "% Identified to Species", x = "Family", fill = "")

famIdByYr <- familyLevYr %>% ungroup() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = family, y = n, fill = famID))+
    geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = T))+
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90 , vjust = 0.5, hjust=1), legend.position = "none")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = famCols)+
  labs(y = "% Identified to Species", x = "Family", fill = "")+
  facet_wrap(~year, ncol = n_distinct(familyLevYr$year)/2)
```



```{r, include=FALSE}
# can check that data going into ecoreg and county is the same

# nrow(datEcoR)
# nrow(datEcoR %>% filter(STUSPS%in% params$state) )

```
\section {Title Page}
Example cover page image 
```{r, out.width="65%", out.height= "65%"}
#knitr::include_graphics("Bombus1_6.jpg" )
```
\newpage
\section {Introduction}

Things to introduce here such as: 

  Bee atlas background
    The Oregon Bee Atlas is a project of the Department of Agriculature at Oregon State University.  
  
  Contributors
  Lincoln Best, Andony Melathopoulos, Antranig Basman, Samuel V. J. Robinson, Rowan Rampton, August Jackson, Olivia - , 
  Other bee atlas members? Caleb, Bonnie
  Anyone else?
  The many volunteers that collect the bees that make this dataset possible, plus those who conduct outreach or support the project in any other way. 
  
  Funders
  This project is funded in part by a USDA NIFA Grant 
  
  Data information
  
  Data is curated by Lincoln Best, Myles Scholz, others?
  
  Use of the data or any component of this report is encouraged, but must acknowledge the authors through citation or acknowledgement. 
  
  Suggested citations:
  
  Report contents: Rampton, Best, Melathopoulos, Jackson, Robinson
  
  Bee atlas data: Best, Melathopoulos, ADD REST , year, other. 
  
  Analysis caveats
  Bee identification is a challenging and time-consuming process. Many bees in the collection have not yet been identified to species. Some very rare species may be present within these unidentified species, but there may also be relatively common bee species that are challenging to identify. As such, some bee-plant interactions may currently be over represented in Ecoregion and County totals. This is likely most applicable to *Bombus* species, nearly all of which are identified to species and are also abundant in the data. Similarly, not all plant species have been identified to species.    
  
\newpage
```{r famID plot, fig.width=7, fig.height= 5, fig.cap= "Proportion of each bee family identified to species, by year"}

famIdByYr

```

```{r genID plot, fig.width=7, fig.height= 7, fig.cap= "Proportion of each bee genus identified to species"}
genusIdedPlot
```

\subsection {Citations}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
citation("tidyverse")
citation()
```

\newpage
\section{State Totals}

```{r, include=FALSE}
sppList <- datSt %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(genSpp) %>% 
  count() 

nSpp <- sppList %>% 
  nrow()

nObs <- datSt %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% nrow()
nCol <- datSt %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(collector) %>% summarise() %>% nrow()
nPlntSpp <- datSt %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(plantGenSpp) %>% summarise() %>% nrow()
  
```

A total of `r nSpp` bee taxa have been identified among specimens submitted by volunteers to the `r params$state` Bee Atlas. There have been a total of `r nObs` bees collected by `r nCol` individual collectors, from `r nPlntSpp` unique plant taxa. 

\section{State Species List - example of first 10 rows for now}
```{r, tab.cap= "State Species List"}
sppList <- slice_head(sppList %>% ungroup(), n = 10 )
kable(sppList)
```


```{r, include=FALSE}
#TODO - DONE - right now species numbers are without genus spp., chao estimates are with genus spp. - need to resolve
 # actually problem is that species numbers exclude missing plants - fixed for now, see notes in renderRmdV3

# make fig cap
```

```{r actFig, include=FALSE}
actP <- actualStateRich(stateChao)+
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", 
                       mid = '#f1eef6',
                       high = '#045a8d')
```
\newpage
\section{State Sampling Needs}
Areas with large differences between estimated species richness and observed species richness are ideal locations for additional sampling. More sampling in these areas has the potential to produce additional species, and improve confidence in measures of species diversity. 

```{r expFig, include=FALSE}
gapP <- gapStateRich(stateChao)+
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "white", 
                       mid = '#ffffcc',
                       high = '#bd0026')

fig1 <- str_c("Counties of ", params$state,".", " Top: Observed Bee Species, Bottom: Expected Bee Species.")

allP <- cowplot::plot_grid(actP, gapP, nrow = 2)
```



```{r fig.height=8, fig.width= 7, fig.cap= fig1 }
allP
```


\newpage
\section {State Counties - Summary}

```{r, include=FALSE}
cSumCap <- paste0("Specimens Collected, Species Present, and Collectors per County in ", params$state, ".")
```

```{r, tab.cap= cSumCap  }
countySummaryTable <- datSt %>% semi_join(allCounty %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% filter(STUSPS %in% params$state), by = c("county" = "altName")) %>%   
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(county) %>% 
  summarise(`Bee Species` = n_distinct(genSpp), 
            `Plant Species` = n_distinct(plantGenSpp), 
            `Bees Collected` = n(), 
            Collectors = n_distinct(collector))

kable(countySummaryTable)
```
\newpage
# By County Summaries
Bee and plant species richness, observed bee-plant interactions, and the plant species/genera that supported the most bee species are summarized below for each county, along with maps of observed and expected species richness. 

Note that some invasive weeds can support significant bee diversity and abundance. When picking plants to support bees, consider replacing exotic species with a functionally equivalent native plant species. Functionally equivalent species should have similar flower structure, similar flower color, and produce flowers during the same part of the year as the exotic species they will be replacing. 

```{r, include=FALSE}
child_section_title <- ""

county2make <- orCounties %>% 
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(altName) %>% summarise() %>%
  pull(altName)
county2make <- county2make[1:3] # TODO - for speed during testing, remove later
```

```{r}
knit_child_with_params_county <- function(x){
  child_section_title <- x
  
  knitr::knit_child(
    "stateCountyChild.Rmd", 
    envir = environment(),
    , quiet = TRUE) }
```

```{r, results = 'asis', warning=FALSE}
resC <- lapply(county2make, knit_child_with_params_county)
```
```{r, results = 'asis',  warning=FALSE}
cat(unlist(resC), sep = "\n")
```
\newpage
\section{State Ecoregions - Summary}

```{r chao for ecoregions, include=FALSE}
# debugonce(addChao2State)
stateChao_ecoreg <- addChao2State(datEcoR, state2do = params$state, varName = name )
```

```{r}
ecoRegArea <- datEcoR %>%   
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(NA_L3NAME) %>% 
  summarise(`Bee Species` = n_distinct(genSpp), 
            `Plant Species` = n_distinct(plantGenSpp), 
            `Bees Collected` = n(), 
            Collectors = n_distinct(collector)) 

kable(ecoRegArea)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
child_section_title <- ""



ecor2make <- 
  stateEcoReg %>% 
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  group_by(name) %>% summarise() %>%
  pull(name)

ecor2make <- ecor2make[1:3] # TODO - for speed during testing, remove later
```

```{r}
knit_child_with_params <- function(x){
  child_section_title <- x
  
  knitr::knit_child(
    "stateEcoregChild.Rmd", 
    envir = environment(),
    , quiet = TRUE) }
```

```{r, results = 'asis', warning=FALSE}
res <- lapply(ecor2make, knit_child_with_params)
```
```{r, results = 'asis',  warning=FALSE}
cat(unlist(res), sep = "\n")
```

```{r}
options(warn=0) #Turn warnings back on
```
